unit: U5 — CLAUDE.md template + composition verification (closes Phase 1.2)

contract.goal: Close Phase 1.2 — ship the project-scoped CLAUDE.md template at plugins/jelly-guild/templates/CLAUDE.md (instantiated per-project by jelly plan; designed for @-import from repo-root CLAUDE.md per Phase 1.1's confirmed mechanism) + a structural-integrity vitest test validating the three-axis decomposition's wiring.
acceptance_criteria: plugins/jelly-guild/templates/CLAUDE.md exists — a project-scoped substrate-posture template with {{PLACEHOLDER}} slots for jelly plan to fill, carrying: substrate declaration, the three-axis dispatch pattern (personality x domain x phase), the available personalities/domains/phases, the dispatch-pattern mapping (whiteboard = parallel x research/planner; evaluator = single x reviewer; generator = single x implementer), the operator-paired PR-review cadence, and pointers to PLAN.md + modes + rubrics.
The template documents that it is @-imported from the repo-root CLAUDE.md (the Phase 1.1 mechanism) — not auto-discovered.

plugins/jelly-guild/three-axis-integrity.test.ts exists — asserts: the 5 personalities + personality-base exist with valid frontmatter; each personality references personality-base.md; the composition mechanism references the modes/domains/ + modes/phases/ paths; the 5 domain modes + 4 phase modes exist; every domain mode has a paired rubric and every rubric has a paired domain (no orphans); the template exists.
The test passes; npm test total rises by the new test count.
rules_applied: RESEARCH.md § Phase 1.1 follow-up (@-import is the propagation mechanism; the template is designed for it; filesystem-walk auto-discovery does NOT work).
Operator decision this unit: permanent structural-integrity test (over verify-at-time or operator-paired /goal probe), located in plugins/jelly-guild/.

User's testing-domain philosophy (which this unit's own mode file encodes): test the boundary, choose the cheapest reliable signal. The structural test catches the real failure class (dangling references, orphan rubrics) without needing /goal.
Panel auto-derivation: evaluator-contract-fit (template) + evaluator-test-unit (the test file).
disqualifiers: The jelly plan verb itself (Phase 1.3 — the template is consumed by it, not the verb).
Editing the personalities / modes / rubrics shipped in U2-U4 (the test validates them; it does not change them).
An operator-paired /goal dispatch probe (deferred to Phase 3.1 dogfood — the structural test is U5's verification).
inputs: projects/2026-05-25-jelly/RESEARCH.md § Phase 1.1 follow-up (the @-import mechanism the template relies on).
The U2-U4 artifacts (agents/*.md, modes/**/*.md, rubrics/*.md) — what the test validates and what the template references.

actions: Wrote plugins/jelly-guild/templates/CLAUDE.md: an HTML-comment header explains jelly plan instantiation + the @-import propagation requirement (literal @projects/{{PROJECT_SLUG}}/CLAUDE.md line + citation to RESEARCH.md § Phase 1.1 follow-up + the subagent-inherits-session-CLAUDE.md note). Body: # {{PROJECT_TITLE}} + {{PROJECT_SLUG}} + {{PROJECT_CONTEXT}} + {{PROJECT_CONVENTIONS}} placeholders; a 'Substrate posture' section with the three-axis dispatch model (names all 5 personalities, 5 domains, 4 phases + an example dispatch brief); a 'Dispatch patterns' section (whiteboard/evaluator/generator); an 'Outcomes grading' section pointing at the rubrics; a 'Review cadence' section (operator-paired, PR-boundary, no auto-merge, no auto-resolve); a 'Pointers' section.

Wrote plugins/jelly-guild/three-axis-integrity.test.ts (42 assertions, 5 describe blocks): file existence (personalities + base + domains + phases + template); personality frontmatter (role, tool superset Read/Glob/Grep/Bash/Write/Edit, model: inherit, base is read-only role personality-base); mcp__jelly__* deferral (asserts tools does NOT contain mcp__jelly__); composition wiring (each personality references personality-base.md; base references modes/domains/ + modes/phases/); domain↔rubric pairing (readdirSync both dirs, filter .md, sort, toEqual — set equality catches orphans either side + a per-domain both-exist check); template (@-import documented, {{PLACEHOLDER}} slots, names the 5 personalities). Mirrors marketplace-manifest.test.ts import + structure conventions; includes a documented minimal flat-key parseFrontmatter helper (deliberately not a full YAML parser — frontmatter is flat; keeps the test dependency-free).

Annotated the readdirSync filter callbacks ((f: string)) to clear the implicit-any TS diagnostic (the node: import diagnostics are repo-wide no-@types/node noise present on every test file and unavoidable without a tsconfig the repo intentionally omits).
Removed plugins/jelly-guild/templates/.gitkeep via git rm — dir now populated.

Verified: npm test → 979 passed across 68 files (was 937; +42 from three-axis-integrity.test.ts). Panel (evaluator-contract-fit + evaluator-test-unit, auto-derived): both approved on first run. The test-unit evaluator spot-checked the domain↔rubric pairing assertion and confirmed it reads two distinct directories (not a tautology) and genuinely catches orphans on either side.

changes_since_previous: Fifth and FINAL unit of Phase 1.2 (jelly-guild substrate). Composes on U1 (scaffold) + U2 (domain modes, WHAT) + U3 (phase modes, WHEN) + U4 (personalities, HOW). With this unit committed, Phase 1.2 is functionally complete: the jelly-guild plugin ships 5 personality subagents + 5 domain modes + 5 paired rubrics + 4 phase modes + a project-scoped CLAUDE.md template, with a structural-integrity test gating the wiring. Phase 1.2's exit criteria are all satisfied. Next: Phase 1.3 (jelly-loom substrate — CLI verbs + manifest.toml + ADR log + MCP server) and Phase 1.3 also picks up the @-line management for the template this unit shipped. Carries lagged pr-opened event for PR #57 in events.jsonl.
notes_for_pr: Closes Phase 1.2. The jelly-guild substrate is complete: three-axis decomposition (5 personalities x 5 domains x 4 phases = additive N+M+P, not multiplicative), 5 paired rubrics for Outcomes grading, and a project-scoped CLAUDE.md template that propagates substrate posture to /goal's fleet via @-import.

Advisory from both panel evaluators (non-gating, surfaced for the record): the integrity test checks domain↔rubric pairing EXISTENCE (set equality of filenames) but not content SYNC. The template + the rubrics both claim 'manually synced in v1; mode is canonical on drift' — nothing automated guards that the rubric's criteria actually track the mode's antipattern catalog. This is within-contract (AC #3 only asked for pair-complete-no-orphans), and the manual-sync tradeoff was an explicit PLAN.md decision. v2 follow-up candidate: derive both mode + rubric from a single source, OR add a content-sync assertion (e.g. every antipattern catalog entry maps to a rubric criterion).

Composition verification scope (operator decision): a permanent structural-integrity test, NOT an operator-paired /goal dispatch probe. Rationale: the failure class worth gating is structural (dangling references, orphan rubrics, malformed frontmatter), which a static test catches cheaply + defends against future drift. True runtime dispatch verification (a jelly personality reading modes under /goal and producing coherent output) is Phase 3.1 dogfood territory — and can't be done in the authoring session anyway (newly-authored agents register next session per L-004).

Template propagation: the template's HTML-comment header documents that it does NOT auto-discover under /goal — it must be @-imported from the repo-root CLAUDE.md (Phase 1.1's confirmed mechanism). Phase 1.3's jelly plan verb manages that @-line. The integrity test asserts the @-import is documented (toContain('@projects/')) so a future edit can't silently drop the propagation note.
Substrate-state carry-over: lagged pr-opened event for PR #57 in events.jsonl rides with this commit per the standard pattern.

After this PR merges, mark Phase 1.2 completed and Phase 1.3 (jelly-loom) becomes the next actionable phase. Phase 1.3 depends on Phase 1.1 (satisfied) — note it does NOT depend on Phase 1.2 per PLAN.md, so it could in principle have run in parallel; it runs next sequentially here.
